file.content:
# Fixture: network-graph
## Task
Small network with ~30 nodes colored by community. Force-directed or circular layout.
## Inputs
- data.json: {nodes[{id, community}], edges[{source,target}], communities}
## Outputs
- figure.pdf, figure.png; caption.tex; gen_figure.py
## Hints
- networkx + matplotlib is fine.
- Avoid hairball: tune edge alpha and node size.

content.text: Now I'll write the generation script and produce the figure.

input.content:
import json
import numpy as np
import matplotlib
matplotlib.use("Agg")
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.patches as mpatches
import networkx as nx

with open("data.json") as f:
    data = json.load(f)

G = nx.Graph()
for node in data["nodes"]:
    G.add_node(node["id"], community=node["community"])
for edge in data["edges"]:
    G.add_edge(edge["source"], edge["target"])

n_communities = data["communities"]
community_map = {node["id"]: node["community"] for node in data["nodes"]}

# Spring layout with seed for reproducibility
pos = nx.spring_layout(G, seed=42, k=1.8 / np.sqrt(len(G.nodes)))

palette = ["#E63946", "#457B9D", "#2A9D8F", "#E9C46A"]
node_colors = [palette[community_map[n]] for n in G.nodes()]

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(7, 7))
fig.patch.set_facecolor("white")
ax.set_facecolor("#F8F9FA")

nx.draw_networkx_edges(
    G, pos, ax=ax,
    alpha=0.25, width=0.8, edge_color="#555555"
)
nx.draw_networkx_nodes(
    G, pos, ax=ax,
    node_color=node_colors, node_size=220,
    linewidths=0.6, edgecolors="white"
)
nx.draw_networkx_labels(
    G, pos, ax=ax,
    font_size=6, font_color="white", font_weight="bold"
)

legend_handles = [
    mpatches.Patch(color=palette[c], label=f"Community {c}")
    for c in range(n_communities)
]
ax.legend(
    handles=legend_handles, loc="lower right",
    framealpha=0.85, fontsize=8, title="Community", title_fontsize=8
)

ax.set_title("Network Graph — Community Structure", fontsize=11, pad=10)
ax.axis("off")
plt.tight_layout()

plt.savefig("figure.pdf", dpi=150, bbox_inches="tight")
plt.savefig("figure.png", dpi=150, bbox_inches="tight")
plt.savefig("figure.svg", bbox_inches="tight")
print("Saved figure.pdf, figure.png, figure.svg")

import numpy as np
import matplotlib
matplotlib.use("Agg")
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.patches as mpatches
import networkx as nx

with open("data.json") as f:
    data = json.load(f)

G = nx.Graph()
for node in data["nodes"]:
    G.add_node(node["id"], community=node["community"])
for edge in data["edges"]:
    G.add_edge(edge["source"], edge["target"])

n_communities = data["communities"]
community_map = {node["id"]: node["community"] for node in data["nodes"]}

# Spring layout with seed for reproducibility
pos = nx.spring_layout(G, seed=42, k=1.8 / np.sqrt(len(G.nodes)))

palette = ["#E63946", "#457B9D", "#2A9D8F", "#E9C46A"]
node_colors = [palette[community_map[n]] for n in G.nodes()]

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(7, 7))
fig.patch.set_facecolor("white")

nx.draw_networkx_edges(
    G, pos, ax=ax,

    G, pos, ax=ax,
    node_color=node_colors, node_size=220,
    linewidths=0.6, edgecolors="white"

nx.draw_networkx_labels(
    G, pos, ax=ax,

legend_handles = [
    mpatches.Patch(color=palette[c], label=f"Community {c}")

ax.legend(
    handles=legend_handles, loc="lower right",

ax.set_title("Network Graph — Community Structure", fontsize=11, pad=10)
ax.axis("off")
plt.tight_layout()

plt.savefig("figure.pdf", dpi=150, bbox_inches="tight")
plt.savefig("figure.png", dpi=150, bbox_inches="tight")

input.content: \caption{Force-directed layout of a 30-node network with 4 communities (nodes colored by community assignment). Edges connect consecutive and cross-community node pairs as specified in \texttt{data.json}. Node positions computed via the Fruchterman--Reingold algorithm (NetworkX spring layout, seed 42); edge opacity tuned to reduce visual clutter.}

- `figure.pdf` and `figure.png` — force-directed network graph, 30 nodes colored by 4 communities
- `figure.svg` — bonus vector output
- `gen_figure.py` — reproducible generation script using NetworkX + matplotlib
- `caption.tex` — LaTeX caption
